description: Recording simulation data over time using the history command in PFC 5.0. Covers FISH function history, wall history, measurement history, ball history, and mechanical history. VERIFIED against PFC 5.0 help docs.

sections.heading: CRITICAL: PFC 5.0 uses 'history add' NOT 'history id'
sections.content:
In PFC 5.0, the history command requires the 'add' primary keyword. The syntax 'history id N @func' (used in some tutorials) is INCORRECT for PFC 5.0.

Correct syntax:
  history add id N fish @fish_function_name
  history add id N mechanical solve unbalanced

Wrong (does not work):
  history id N @fish_function_name        ; ERROR: 'id' is not a primary keyword
  history id N mechanical solve unbalanced  ; ERROR

Primary keywords for 'history': add | delete | dump | hist_rep | label | limits | list | ncycle | nstep | purge | reset | write

sections.heading: FISH Function History
sections.content:
Record the return value of a FISH function at each timestep.

Syntax:
  history add id N fish @fish_function_name

  id N                  — unique history ID
  @fish_function_name   — name of a FISH function (must return a value)
  fish                  — literal keyword indicating FISH function follows

Example:
  ; Define a FISH function
  define _get_stress
    _get_stress = (math.abs(wall.force.contact(wall.find(6),3)) + \
                   math.abs(wall.force.contact(wall.find(5),3))) / (2.0*_ca)
  end

  ; Record it every step
  history add id 1 fish @_get_stress

The history records whatever value the named FISH function assigns to its own name (the return value convention in FISH).

sections.heading: Mechanical History (Unbalanced Force)
sections.content:
Record global mechanical state variables.

Syntax:
  history add id N mechanical solve <quantity>

Available quantities:
  unbalanced  — maximum unbalanced force magnitude
  aratio      — average ratio (unbalanced/contact force, for solve convergence)
  lratio      — local ratio (for solve convergence)
  mratio      — maximum ratio (for solve convergence)
  age         — total mechanical age
  time        — mechanical solve time
  totalcycles — total number of mechanical cycles
  cycle       — mechanical cycle number

Example:
  history add id 2 mechanical solve unbalanced

NOTE: 'mechanical unbalanced' (without 'solve') is INCORRECT. The full chain must be:
  history add id N mechanical solve unbalanced
  NOT: history add id N mechanical unbalanced

sections.heading: Wall History
sections.content:
Record wall quantities directly without defining a FISH function.

Syntax (PFC 5.0):
  history add id N wall <quantity> id M

Available quantities:
  xcontactforce   — total x-contact force on wall ID M
  ycontactforce   — total y-contact force on wall ID M
  zcontactforce   — total z-contact force on wall ID M
  xdisplacement   — x-displacement of wall ID M
  ydisplacement   — y-displacement of wall ID M
  zdisplacement   — z-displacement of wall ID M

Examples:
  history add id 10 wall xcontactforce id 3
  history add id 11 wall zcontactforce id 6
  history add id 12 wall zdisplacement id 6

sections.heading: History Write (Data Export)
sections.content:
Write history data to a file for post-processing.

Syntax:
  history write <id1> <id2> ... [file 'filename']

  IDs are space-separated (NOT comma-separated)
  The 'file' keyword is optional; default writes to 'pfc3d.his' or 'pfc2d.his'

Examples:
  history write 1 2 file 'biaxial_results.his'
  history write 1 2 3
  history write file 'all_histories.his'

Wrong:
  history write id 1,2 file 'results.his'    ; comma not valid, 'id' keyword not valid here

sections.heading: Controlling Recording Frequency
sections.content:
Control how often histories are recorded.

Syntax:
  set hist_rep N

  N   — record every N steps (default: 1 = every step)

Example:
  set hist_rep 10     ; record history every 10 steps

To reduce data volume during long runs:
  set hist_rep 100

sections.heading: Practical Notes
sections.content:
- Always use 'history add' as the primary keyword. 'history id' is NOT valid PFC 5.0.
- For 'mechanical' history, the full chain is: 'history add id N mechanical solve <quantity>'.
- History write uses space-separated IDs: 'history write 1 2 3 file "data.his"'.
- Default history output filename is pfc3d.his (3D) or pfc2d.his (2D).
- History IDs must be unique; reusing an ID overwrites the old history.
- For complex derived quantities (stress ratio, etc.), define a FISH function and use 'history add id N fish @func'.
